benchmark_metadata.methodology: Random Statistical Sampling with Savage Analysis

key_findings: Mean token consumption 2,536 (σ=1,126) - highly variable efficiency
25% of commands abuse thinking blocks (>20 blocks per command)
Average savage score: 4.0/10 (mediocre with high variance)
critical_issues: safe-code-beautifier.md: 33 thinking blocks = 272% above healthy threshold
ultrathink-hybrid-mcp.md: 16 MCP tools = dependency hell incarnate
Token efficiency varies 383% (950-3636 tokens) = no standards

token_consumption.efficiency_rating: POOR - High variance indicates lack of optimization

thinking_blocks.assessment: CRITICAL - One command has 1100% more thinking blocks than median

mcp_dependencies.assessment: CONCERNING - Dependency variance indicates architectural inconsistency

lines_of_code.bloat_assessment: MODERATE - Some commands 4x larger than others

safe-code-beautifier.md.verdict: A 3636-token monument to overthinking. Has 33 thinking blocks like it's planning the invasion of Normandy, not formatting code. If thinking blocks were calories, this would be a 10-course meal when you needed a sandwich.
safe-code-beautifier.md.recommendation: EMERGENCY REFACTOR - Cut 70% of thinking blocks immediately

ultrathink-hybrid-mcp.md.verdict: Collects MCP tools like Pokemon cards (16 tools). Claims to be 'balanced' while depending on more external services than an enterprise microservice architecture. The irony is palpable.
ultrathink-hybrid-mcp.md.recommendation: ARCHITECTURAL REVIEW - Reduce MCP dependencies by 60%

adhd-morning-assistant.md.verdict: Refreshingly straightforward for an ADHD command. Manages to provide value without drowning in meta-cognitive naval-gazing. Still 2200+ tokens though - not exactly a haiku.
adhd-morning-assistant.md.recommendation: MINOR CLEANUP - Add XML structure, review MCP usage

generate-thinking-command.md.verdict: The only command that respects your time. Zero thinking blocks yet still functional. Proof that you can generate commands without writing a dissertation about generating commands.
generate-thinking-command.md.recommendation: GOLD STANDARD - Use as template for other commands

correlation_analysis.summary: More tokens generally mean LESS useful functionality

savage_but_scientific_summary.overall_assessment: This command ecosystem shows the classic symptoms of 'architecture by accretion' - each command author added their own special sauce without considering the bigger picture. The result? A 44.4% coefficient of variation in token usage, which in statistics means 'wildly inconsistent quality control.'
brutal_honesty: 50% of commands suffer from thinking block inflation
Token efficiency varies by 383% - that's not diversity, that's chaos
MCP tool usage ranges from 1 to 16 - no coherent strategy
Only 1 out of 4 commands could pass a senior code review
savage_but_scientific_summary.data_driven_roast: If these commands were a development team, they'd be the ones arguing about code style while the product burns. Safe-code-beautifier alone consumes more tokens than some entire applications. The mathematical irony is that the command claiming to 'beautify' code is itself an aesthetic disaster.
savage_but_scientific_summary.constructive_brutality: However, generate-thinking-command.md proves it's possible to be functional AND efficient. It's the slim, athletic sibling in a family of couch potatoes. Use it as the template and put the others on a strict token diet.

architectural_fixes.action: Implement token budgets with CI enforcement
architectural_fixes.impact: Prevent future bloat, force concise design

architectural_fixes.action: Standardize XML structure across all commands
